claim: AppBootstrapper's blanket catch{} around module initializers should become fail-fast by default (a module registration failure should crash the host loudly), with an explicit opt-in degraded-boot mode; and the framework should introduce a small KoanException hierarchy (e.g. KoanBootException, KoanDataException wrapping provider errors with config-key hints).
corrections: SCOPE: 'blanket' over-reaches. AppBootstrapper has two distinct leniency classes. Assembly-closure load failures (AppBootstrapper.cs:60,72,92,95) MUST stay lenient — Assembly.Load of referenced names routinely fails for legitimate reasons (ref-only/trimmed/platform-specific assemblies); this is the analog of Spring Boot's @ConditionalOnClass silent-skip and of ReflectionTypeLoadException partial-results handling (RegistryManifestLoader.cs:66-74,121-127). Fail-fast applies ONLY to the initializer-execution catch (AppBootstrapper.cs:112-123) and the CreateManifestInvoker outer swallow (AppBootstrapper.cs:237-243, which can silently no-op the ENTIRE framework). Precedent split confirmed across ASP.NET (.NET 6 moved BackgroundService from Ignore to StopHost; ConfigureServices/IStartupFilter/StartAsync exceptions crash the host), Spring Boot (activated auto-config exception = fatal context-refresh failure with FailureAnalyzer report; missing class = silent skip), Rails (initializer raise aborts boot): lenient on absence, fatal on broken execution.

The KoanException HIERARCHY half is refuted. The codebase has ~30 specific sealed exception types with no common base (only the SecretException mini-family); ARCH-0084 canonizes CapabilityNotSupportedException as 'the one fail-loud path'; AdapterResolver/resolution messages already embed exact config keys (AdapterResolver.cs:40-47,76-78) — message-enrichment at throw sites IS the established pattern. KoanDataException wrapping provider errors would touch the data hot path across 11+ connectors (1,548 'throw new' sites in src), break user catch(NpgsqlException) compatibility, and contradict the documented ARCH-0080 stance that connectivity is a runtime concern surfaced via health contributors (Redis KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:67-72) — and its target gap (raw driver exception at first use) is already half-covered by per-adapter IHealthContributor + StartupProbeService + connection-string provenance with source-key in the boot report. Keep exactly ONE new sealed type: KoanBootException(moduleType, assembly, version, phase, inner) — justified solely because no ILogger exists at InitializeModules time, so the exception is the diagnostic channel and must name the failing module.

MECHANISM: the opt-out cannot be IOptions-based — InitializeModules runs on IServiceCollection before the provider/configuration exist. It must be an env var (precedent: KOAN_VERBOSE_ASSEMBLIES read at AppBootstrapper.cs:25) and/or an AddKoan(Action<KoanBootstrapOptions>) parameter; failure logging must go to Console/stderr (precedent: EmitAssemblySummary uses Console.WriteLine) plus the existing RegistrySummarySnapshot→boot-report channel (AppRuntime.cs:67-73), which is the 'partial mechanism that just isn't wired'.

The claim misses that this is not a new policy decision: the framework ALREADY adopted fail-fast-by-default for boot-time work in KoanBackgroundServiceOrchestrator (FailFastOnStartupFailure=true, per-service ContinueOnFailure opt-out, unconditional LogError — KoanBackgroundServiceOrchestrator.cs:21,159-171; default set at KoanBackgroundServiceAutoRegistrar.cs:26). AppBootstrapper is an internal inconsistency with established canon (and with ARCH-0084 'fail-loud'), which makes the change a consistency fix, not a philosophy change — a materially stronger justification.

AppRuntime provenance/Describe swallows (AppRuntime.cs:34,61-64,109,130-133) should STAY best-effort (boot-report cosmetics must never block start) but should mark the module's provenance entry as report-failed instead of vanishing — degraded-boot visibility, not fail-fast.

Per-module opt-out granularity should mirror the startup-service pattern: modules whose registration is genuinely optional declare it ([ContinueOnFailure]-style or KoanModule virtual), rather than a single global degraded switch being the only escape hatch.
evidence: src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Bootstrap/AppBootstrapper.cs:110-124 — initializer loop; empty catch '// best-effort to avoid failing the host during bootstrap'; no logging of any kind (file's only output channel is Console.WriteLine, used for assembly summary at :151)

src/Koan.Core/IKoanAutoRegistrar.cs:7 — IKoanAutoRegistrar : IKoanInitializer, so EVERY module's Register/Initialize runs inside that swallowed loop; KoanModule.cs:52-58 routes the new ARCH-0086 primitive through the same path
src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Bootstrap/AppBootstrapper.cs:60,72,92,95 — assembly-closure catches (GetReferencedAssemblies/Assembly.Load/bad files): the legitimately-lenient tier

src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Bootstrap/AppBootstrapper.cs:227-245 — CreateManifestInvoker swallows reflection failure of Koan.Core's own RegistryManifestLoader; if hit, nothing is ever discovered and AddKoan() silently does nothing

src/Koan.Core/BackgroundServices/KoanBackgroundServiceOrchestrator.cs:21,159-171 + KoanBackgroundServiceAutoRegistrar.cs:26 — existing in-codebase fail-fast canon: FailFastOnStartupFailure=true default, [KoanStartupService(ContinueOnFailure=true)] per-service opt-out, LogError always

src/Koan.Data.AI/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:147-169 — deliberate, actionable InvalidOperationExceptions thrown INSIDE Initialize ([Embedding] misuse) that the AppBootstrapper catch currently eats silently: module authors already assume fail-fast semantics the bootstrapper denies them

src/Connectors/Data/Redis/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:53-128 (esp. 67-72) — ARCH-0080 policy comment: 'The factory must NEVER throw at host-build time — a missing/unreachable Redis is a runtime concern (health contributors...)'; connectivity throws live in DI factories, NOT Initialize — proving Initialize-time exceptions are always bugs, never operational noise
src/Connectors/Data/Postgres/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:32-55 — representative registrar Initialize: pure services.Add* calls, zero I/O

src/Koan.Data.Core/AdapterResolver.cs:40-47,76-78 — established message-enrichment pattern: failures name exact config keys ('Check Koan:Data:Sources:{name} configuration', 'Ensure adapter packages are referenced and AddKoan() has been called')

Exception census (grep 'class \w*Exception' across src): ~30 specific sealed types, no common KoanException base; only SecretException (src/Koan.Secrets.Abstractions) forms a family; docs/decisions/ARCH-0084-unified-capability-model.md:64,103 names CapabilityNotSupportedException 'the one fail-loud path'
grep count: 1,548 'throw new' sites across src — the wrapping surface a KoanDataException would imply

src/Koan.Core/Hosting/Runtime/AppRuntime.cs:67-73,112-135 — RegistrySummarySnapshot already feeds the boot report; CollectProvenance swallows Describe() failures per-module (best-effort, correctly so)

src/Koan.Core/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:23-30 — AddKoan calls InitializeModules on IServiceCollection before provider build: no ILogger/IConfiguration available, constraining the opt-out mechanism to env var / AddKoan parameter

docs/assessment/02-philosophy-dx.md:110-124 — assessment source: 243 empty/comment-only catches, 'module vanishes... developer meets it later as Unable to resolve service', and the concession 'when resolution fails, messages are excellent'
docs/decisions/CORE-0003-initializer-idempotency.md — the always-run-initializers decision the swallow rides on; it mandates idempotency, not silence
tests/: grep found no test relying on swallowed initializer failures
refinedRecommendation: Split AppBootstrapper's leniency into two tiers and align the executing tier with the framework's own existing canon. TIER A (stay lenient, unchanged semantics): assembly-closure loads (AppBootstrapper.cs:60,72,92,95) and per-type manifest-scan skips (RegistryManifestLoader) — absence of a loadable dependency is never fatal (Spring @ConditionalOnClass / ReflectionTypeLoadException precedent); add their counts to the KOAN_VERBOSE_ASSEMBLIES output. TIER B (fail-fast by default): an exception escaping initializer construction or Initialize()/Register() (AppBootstrapper.cs:112-123), and failure of the manifest-invoker itself (AppBootstrapper.cs:227-245), is rethrown wrapped in a single new sealed KoanBootException carrying {module type, assembly+version, boot phase, inner} — mirroring KoanBackgroundServiceOrchestrator.FailFastOnStartupFailure=true (the policy the framework already shipped for startup services) and .NET 6's BackgroundService StopHost migration. Escape hatches, in precedence order: per-module opt-out (KoanModule virtual ContinueOnRegistrationFailure / attribute, mirroring [KoanStartupService(ContinueOnFailure=true)]) for genuinely optional modules, then a global degraded-boot switch KOAN_BOOT_LENIENT=1 or AddKoan(o => o.LenientBoot = true) — env-var/parameter based because no IConfiguration exists at this point. Logging requirement in BOTH modes: Console.Error.WriteLine the module name + full exception (the file's only available channel, per EmitAssemblySummary precedent) AND record the failure in RegistrySummarySnapshot so the existing boot report (AppRuntime.cs:67-73) renders a MODULES-FAILED block; in lenient mode the host additionally marks the module's provenance entry failed instead of letting it vanish. Do NOT introduce a KoanException hierarchy or KoanDataException provider-wrapping: the established and assessment-praised pattern is specific sealed exceptions plus config-key-bearing messages at resolution boundaries (AdapterResolver), ARCH-0084 already designates the one fail-loud path, ARCH-0080 deliberately keeps connectivity errors out of boot, and wrapping would disturb 11+ connectors' hot paths (1,548 throw sites) while breaking user catches of provider exceptions — instead, keep enriching messages at the few resolution/connection-open boundaries that lack hints. Smallest change that fixes the silent-vanish bug class TODAY (~20 lines, one file): in the AppBootstrapper.cs:112-123 catch, write module type + exception to Console.Error, append to a failures list surfaced via RegistrySummarySnapshot, and rethrow as KoanBootException unless KOAN_BOOT_LENIENT=1.
